purpose: Assesses financial fragility by sector rather than in aggregate, identifying which sectors are in hedge, speculative, or Ponzi financing positions and how crisis propagates through the sectoral structure
components: Household balance sheet assessment (DSR, mortgage lock-in, net worth)
Covenant-lite impact on Minsky phase transitions (elimination of speculative phase)
Cross-sector transmission channels (corporate crisis with household insulation)
current_assessment: Households firmly in hedge position (DSR 9.7%, 95% locked mortgages). Corporate sector transitioning from speculative to Ponzi at the margin (40%+ unprofitable, declining hedge share from ~70% to ~55-60%). Novel crisis topology implies 2001-like sharp-but-shallow downturn. No clean historical precedent for this bifurcation.
limitations: No historical precedent for this exact bifurcation makes outcome prediction unreliable
Private credit opacity ($1.8T) obscures true corporate financing position
Threshold for corporate distress breaching household insulation is unknown
The framework does not account for non-financial transmission channels (confidence, employment)
